intro: Hymenidium album, also known as Peruvian mint or Cat's whisker, is a plant species native to the Andean region of South America, characterized by its white flowers and hairy leaves. Traditionally, Hymenidium album has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antioxidant effects, providing relief from digestive issues, fever, and respiratory problems. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties. Hymenidium album is commonly prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used as a flavoring agent in herbal remedies and beverages, such as Peruvian mint tea.
